mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, increasing present values of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity repricing within the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand growth that drives corporate revenue forecasts upward, producing positive equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses predictive power with t-stat below 1.5 or forward quarterly returns turn negative when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, supporting corporate margins and generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate household income and consumer expenditure capacity, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward out-of-sample R-squared remains below 0.01 or payroll growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.3% signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets produce positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of high-inflation threshold breaches without subsequent rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates structural demand shifts that force earnings revisions, where GDP outpacing retail sales predicts negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The interaction effect yields a negative Rank IC or fails to separate return distributions at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation expectations rise, liquidity injections fail to stimulate real activity, causing risk-off flows and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL is rising.
